info.title: LiteLLM API
info.description: Complete reference for all LiteLLM API endpoints. LiteLLM is a unified interface for 100+ LLM providers — OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, AWS Bedrock, Azure, Cohere, and more.

tags.name: Chat & Text
tags.description: Chat completions, text completions, and response APIs

tags.name: Images & Video
tags.description: Image generation, editing, and variation endpoints

tags.name: Vector Stores & RAG
tags.description: Vector store management and semantic search

tags.name: Web Search
tags.description: Transparent server-side web search execution for any LLM provider. LiteLLM intercepts `litellm_web_search` tool calls and executes them using your configured search provider (Perplexity, Tavily, Exa, etc.), then returns a single final answer — no extra client-side logic needed.

post.description: Creates a model response for the given chat conversation. Supports 100+ LLM providers via a unified OpenAI-compatible interface.

messages.content: What is the weather in Paris?

function.name: get_weather
function.description: Get current weather for a city

web_search.description: Include the `litellm_web_search` tool — LiteLLM intercepts the tool call, executes the search against your configured provider, and returns a single final answer.

messages.content: What are the latest AI model releases this week?

function.name: litellm_web_search
function.description: Search the web for current information

post.description: Creates a completion for the provided prompt. Legacy text completion endpoint, compatible with OpenAI's /completions API.
Creates a model response using the OpenAI /responses API format. Supports multi-turn conversations, extended thinking, and tool use.
Transcribes audio into text. Compatible with OpenAI Whisper and other speech-to-text providers.
Generates audio from the input text. Supports OpenAI TTS, ElevenLabs, and other text-to-speech providers.
Creates an image given a prompt. Supports DALL-E 3, Stable Diffusion, and other image generation providers.
Creates an edited or extended image given an original image and a text prompt.
Creates a variation of a given image.
Creates an embedding vector representing the input text. Supports 50+ embedding providers including OpenAI, Cohere, Voyage AI, and more.

value.input: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog

post.description: Reranks a list of documents by relevance to a query. Supports Cohere, Voyage AI, Jina AI, and other reranking providers.

value.query: What is the capital of France?
documents: Paris is the capital of France.
London is the capital of the UK.
The Eiffel Tower is in Paris.

post.description: Creates a vector store for storing and searching embeddings from files.

file_id.description: ID of the file to attach

post.description: Performs semantic search over files in a vector store.
Upload a file for use with fine-tuning, batch operations, or assistants.

get.description: Returns a list of files that have been uploaded.

post.description: Creates an assistant with a model, instructions, and optional tools.
Creates a new conversation thread.

messages.description: Optional initial messages to seed the thread

post.description: Executes an assistant against a thread and produces a response.

model.description: Override the assistant's model for this run

instructions.description: Override the assistant's instructions for this run

post.description: Creates and executes a batch from an uploaded JSONL file of requests. Async processing — up to 50% cheaper than real-time requests.
Creates a fine-tuning job that trains a model on your data.
Creates an evaluation configuration to test model performance against a dataset and grading criteria.
Executes an evaluation against a model and data source.

data_source.description: Data source configuration for this run

post.description: Classifies if text and/or images are potentially harmful. OpenAI-compatible moderation endpoint.
Applies a named guardrail to text for safety filtering, PII detection, or content policy enforcement.

response_text.description: Processed text after applying the guardrail

post.description: Extracts and structures text from documents and images using OCR. Returns structured Markdown output per page.

get.description: Lists all models available on this LiteLLM proxy, including models from all configured providers.

post.description:
Creates a chat completion with automatic web search execution. Include the `litellm_web_search` function in `tools` — when the model triggers it, LiteLLM executes the search using your configured provider and returns the final answer in a single response.

**Supported search providers:** Perplexity, Tavily, Exa AI, Parallel AI, Google PSE, DataForSEO, Firecrawl, SearXNG, Linkup.

Configure via `config.yaml`:
```yaml
litellm_settings:
  callbacks:
    - websearch_interception:
        enabled_providers: [openai, anthropic, mistral]
        search_tool_name: perplexity-search

search_tools:
  - search_tool_name: perplexity-search
    litellm_params:
      search_provider: perplexity
      api_key: os.environ/PERPLEXITY_API_KEY
```

See [Web Search Integration docs](/docs/integrations/websearch_interception) for full configuration.

example.content: What is the latest news on LLM releases?

tools.description: Include the `litellm_web_search` function definition to enable automatic web search execution

function.name: litellm_web_search
function.description: Search the web for current information

tool_choice.description: Controls which tool the model calls. Defaults to `auto`

stream.description: Stream SSE delta events. Defaults to `false`

max_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate

messages.content: What is the latest news on LLM releases?

function.name: litellm_web_search
function.description: Search the web for current information

200.description: A single final chat completion response. The intermediate web search tool call and result are handled internally — your application always receives a clean completion object.

message.content: Here are the latest LLM releases as of today: OpenAI released GPT-4o with improved multimodal capabilities, Anthropic launched Claude 3.5 Sonnet with extended context, and Google DeepMind announced Gemini 2.0 Flash with real-time capabilities...

401.description: Unauthorized — invalid or missing API key

BearerAuth.description: Pass your LiteLLM API key as a Bearer token. Generate keys via the LiteLLM UI or `POST /key/generate`.

Limit.name: limit
Limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

After.name: after
After.description: Cursor for pagination — ID of the last item from the previous page

BadRequest.description: Bad request — invalid or missing parameters

Unauthorized.description: Unauthorized — invalid or missing API key

parameters.description: JSON Schema object describing the function parameters

model.description: Model ID. Supports any provider prefix — e.g. `gpt-4o`, `claude-opus-4-6`, `gemini/gemini-2.0-flash`, `bedrock/anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet`, `azure/gpt-4o`.

messages.description: List of messages comprising the conversation

max_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate

stream.description: If true, returns a stream of SSE delta events

n.description: Number of chat completion choices to generate

tools.description: List of tools the model may call

logit_bias.description: Token ID to bias score mapping

user.description: Unique end-user identifier for rate limiting and logging

metadata.description: LiteLLM-specific metadata for logging, routing, and tagging

message.content: Hello! How can I help you today?

input.description: Text prompt or array of input items

file.description: Audio file to transcribe (mp3, mp4, mpeg, mpga, m4a, wav, webm — max 25MB)

language.description: ISO-639-1 language code (e.g. 'en', 'fr'). Auto-detected if omitted.

model.description: TTS model. Use `tts-1` (fast) or `tts-1-hd` (high quality) for OpenAI.

prompt.description: Text description of the desired image (max 4000 chars for DALL-E 3)

image.description: PNG image to edit (max 4MB, must have alpha channel for DALL-E 2)

mask.description: Optional mask PNG — white areas indicate where to edit

url.description: URL of the generated image (expires after 1 hour)

revised_prompt.description: Prompt rewritten by DALL-E 3 for safety/quality

input.description: Text or array of texts to embed

dimensions.description: Output embedding dimensions (supported by select models)

query.description: Search query to rank documents against

documents.description: Documents (strings or objects with 'text' field) to rerank

top_n.description: Number of top results to return. Defaults to all.

index.description: Original index in the input documents array

max_num_results.description: Maximum number of chunks to return

rewrite_query.description: Use LLM to rewrite query for better retrieval

tools.description: Tools the assistant can use (code_interpreter, file_search, function)

input_file_id.description: ID of an uploaded JSONL file. Each line is a request object with `custom_id`, `method`, `url`, and `body`.

endpoint.description: API endpoint to call for each request in the batch

completion_window.description: Time window for batch to complete

training_file.description: File ID of training data in JSONL chat format

validation_file.description: Optional File ID for validation data

suffix.description: Suffix appended to the fine-tuned model name

fine_tuned_model.description: Name of the resulting fine-tuned model (when status=succeeded)

data_source_config.description: Configuration for data source (stored_completions, custom, jsonl, etc.)

guardrail_name.description: Name of the guardrail (as configured in litellm_settings.guardrails)

text.description: Text to run through the guardrail

language.description: ISO language code for language-specific guardrails

entities.description: Entity types to detect (e.g. PERSON, EMAIL, PHONE_NUMBER)

url.description: URL of the document or image

include_image_base64.description: Include extracted image data in response

image_limit.description: Maximum number of images to extract per page

image_min_size.description: Minimum image size in pixels to extract
